title: CUT&RUN Abbreviated Protocol
author: Derek Janssens
materials: Rotate 5-10 min at room temperature. 00:10:00, Rotation

steps.title: Harvest fresh culture(s) at room temperature and count cells
steps.instruction: Harvest fresh culture(s) at room temperature and count cells. The same protocol can be used for up to 500,000 mammalian cells per sample and/or digestion time point.

steps.title: Centrifuge 3 min 600 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 3 min 600 x g at room temperature and withdraw liquid. 00:03:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Resuspend in 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 1.5 mL room temperature Wash buffer by gently pipetting and transfer if necessary to a 2 mL tube. undetermined, Wash buffer

steps.title: Place on the magnet stand to...
steps.instruction: Place on the magnet stand to clear and pull off the liquid.

steps.title: Place each tube at a low...
steps.instruction: Place each tube at a low angle on the vortex mixer set to low (~1100 rpm) and squirt 50-150 μL of the Antibody buffer per sample along the side while gently vortexing to allow the solution to dislodge most or all of the beads. Tap to dislodge the remaining beads 150 µL, Antibody buffer

steps.title: Place on a nutator or tube...
steps.instruction: Place on a nutator or tube rotator at room temperature for ~2 hr or at 4 °C overnight. 02:00:00, Nutator or tube rotator at RT

steps.title: Place each tube at a low...
steps.instruction: Place each tube at a low angle on the vortex mixer set to low (~1100 rpm). Squirt 150 μL of the Protein A-MNase or Protein A/G-MNase fusion protein at 700 ng/mL (e.g., 1:200 of a 140 µg/mL glycerol stock) in Dig-wash buffer (per sample and/or digestion time point) along the side while gently vortexing to allow the solution to dislodge most or all of the beads. Tap to dislodge the remaining beads. undetermined, Protein A-MNase or Protein A/G-MNase (700 ng/mL) in Dig-wash buffer

steps.title: Place on the nutator or tube...
steps.instruction: Place on the nutator or tube rotator at 4 °C for ~1 hr. 01:00:00, Nutator or tube rotator at 4 °C

steps.title: To each sample add 2 μL 10% SDS (to 0
steps.instruction: To each sample add 2 μL 10% SDS (to 0.1%), and 2.5 μL Proteinase K (20 mg/ml). Mix by inversion and incubate 1 hr @ 50 °C. undetermined, 10% SDS (to 0.1%)/sample undetermined, Proteinase K (20 mg/ml)/sample Undetermined, Incubation 01:00:00, Incubation

steps.title: Add an equal volume of Phenol...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of Phenol Chloroform to the sample (e.g. to 200 µL sample add 200 µL Phenol Chloroform). Mix by full-speed vortexing ~2 s. undetermined, PCl 00:00:02, Vortexing

steps.title: Transfer to a phase-lock tube (e
steps.instruction: Transfer to a phase-lock tube (e.g., Qiagen MaXtract), and centrifuge 5 min, room temperature @ 16,000 x g. 00:05:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Add an equivalent volume of chloroform...
steps.instruction: Add an equivalent volume of chloroform to the initial sample volume (e.g. for a 200 µL starting sample volume add 200 µL chloroform). Invert ~10X to mix and then centrifuge 5 min, room temperature @ 16,000 x g. undetermined, Chloroform 00:05:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Remove the top liquid phase by...
steps.instruction: Remove the top liquid phase by pipetting to a fresh tube containing 2 μL 2 mg/mL glycogen (diluted 1:10 from 20 mg/mL glycogen stock). undetermined, 2 mg/ml glycogen

steps.title: Add 500 μL 100% ethanol and...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL 100% ethanol and mix by vortexing or tube inversion. undetermined, 100% ethanol

steps.title: Chill on ice and centrifuge 10...
steps.instruction: Chill on ice and centrifuge 10 min, 4 °C @ 16,000 x g. 00:10:00, Centrifugation Undetermined, Centrifugation

steps.title: Pour off the liquid and drain on a paper towel
steps.instruction: Pour off the liquid and drain on a paper towel.

steps.title: Rinse the pellet in 1 ml...
steps.instruction: Rinse the pellet in 1 ml 100% ethanol and centrifuge 1 min, 4 °C @ 16,000 x g. undetermined, 100% ethanol 00:01:00, Centrifugation Undetermined, Centrifugation

steps.title: Carefully pour off the liquid and...
steps.instruction: Carefully pour off the liquid and drain on a paper towel. Air dry. 00:05:00, Air Dry

steps.title: When the pellet is dry, dissolve...
steps.instruction: When the pellet is dry, dissolve in 30-50 μL 1 mM Tris-HCl pH 8 0.1 mM EDTA, then transfer to a new 0.6 mL Lo-Bind microcentrifuge tube. undetermined, 1 mM Tris-HCl pH 8 0.1 mM EDTA

steps.title: Quantify library yield using dsDNA-specific assay, such as Qubit
steps.instruction: Quantify library yield using dsDNA-specific assay, such as Qubit.

steps.title: Determine the size distribution of libraries...
steps.instruction: Determine the size distribution of libraries by Agilent 4200 TapeStation analysis.

steps.title: Pool samples at equimolar concentrations and...
steps.instruction: Pool samples at equimolar concentrations and perform paired-end Illumina sequencing on the barcoded libraries following the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: We align paired-end reads using Bowtie2 version 2
steps.instruction: We align paired-end reads using Bowtie2 version 2.2.5 with options: --local --very-sensitive- local --no-unal --no-mixed --no-discordant --phred33 -I 10 -X 700. For mapping spike-in fragments, we also use the --no-overlap --no-dovetail options to avoid cross-mapping of the experimental genome to that of the spike-in DNA. CRITICAL STEP: Separation of sequenced fragments into ≤120 bp and ≥150 bp size classes provides mapping of the local vicinity of a DNA-binding protein, but this can vary depending on the steric access to the DNA by the tethered MNase. Single-end sequencing is not recommended for CUT&RUN, as it sacrifices resolution and discrimination between transcription factors and neighboring nucleosomes.

steps.title: Scripts are available from https://github
steps.instruction: Scripts are available from https://github.com/Henikoff/Cut-and-Run for spike-in calibration and for peak-calling.

steps.title: Centrifuge 3 min 600 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 3 min 600 x g at room temperature and withdraw liquid. 00:03:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: While gently vortexing the cells at...
steps.instruction: While gently vortexing the cells at room temperature, add the ConA-coated magnetic bead slurry.

steps.title: Repeat steps 3 and 4 two more times
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 3 and 4 two more times. go to step #1 CRITICAL STEP: Thorough washing removes free sugars and other molecules that can compete for binding to the Concanavalin A coated-beads, ensuring efficient binding and recovery of the cells of interest.

steps.title: Resuspend in 1 mL room temperature...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 1 mL room temperature Wash Buffer by gently pipetting.

steps.title: Mix well by vigorous inversion to...
steps.instruction: Mix well by vigorous inversion to ensure the bead-bound cells are in a homogenous suspension and divide into aliquots in 1.5-mL tubes, one for each antibody to be used. NOTE: Some users have experienced issues with ConA beads sticking to the sides of the tube and coming out of solution during antibody incubation steps. In this case, 0.6-mL Lo-bind microcentrifuge tubes can be used, and subsequent Dig-wash volumes reduced to 300 µL. However, when bound to low cell numbers (e.g. <100K) the ConA beads will be extremely slippery on the sides of lo-bind tubes, and so great care must be taken while removing solutions on the magnet during wash steps etc. to avoid losing the sample.

steps.title: Mix in the primary antibody to...
steps.instruction: Mix in the primary antibody to a final concentration of 1:100 or to the manufacturer’s recommended concentration for immunofluorescence.

steps.title: Remove liquid from the cap and...
steps.instruction: Remove liquid from the cap and side with a quick pulse on a micro-centrifuge. Place on the magnet stand to clear (~30 s) and pull off all of the liquid. 00:00:30, Magnet stand

steps.title: Remove liquid from the cap and...
steps.instruction: Remove liquid from the cap and side with a quick pulse on a micro-centrifuge. Place on the magnet stand (~30 sec) to clear and pull off all of the liquid. 00:00:30, Magnet stand

steps.title: Add 1 mL Dig-Wash buffer, mix...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL Dig-Wash buffer, mix by inversion, or by gentle pipetting if clumps persist. 1 mL, Dig-Wash buffer

steps.title: Repeat Dig-wash steps 21-22
steps.instruction: Repeat Dig-wash steps 21-22. go to step #1

steps.title: Place each tube at a low...
steps.instruction: Place each tube at a low angle on the vortex mixer set to low (~1100 rpm) and add 100 μL of the Dig-wash buffer (per sample and/or digestion time point) along the side while gently vortexing to allow the solution to dislodge most or all of the beads. Tap to dislodge the remaining beads. 100 µL, Dig-wash buffer (per sample or digestion time point)

steps.title: Remove liquid from the cap and...
steps.instruction: Remove liquid from the cap and side with a quick pulse on a micro-centrifuge. Place on the magnet stand (~30 s) to clear and pull off all of the liquid. 00:00:30, Magnet stand

steps.title: Add 1 mL Dig-wash buffer, mix...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL Dig-wash buffer, mix by inversion, or by gentle pipetting if clumps persist. 1 mL, Dig-wash buffer

steps.title: Repeat Dig-wash steps 27-28
steps.instruction: Repeat Dig-wash steps 27-28. go to step #1

steps.title: Insert tubes into the 1
steps.instruction: Insert tubes into the 1.5 mL wells of a heater block sitting in wet ice to chill down to 0 °C. 0 °C

steps.title: Remove each tube from the block,...
steps.instruction: Remove each tube from the block, mix in 2 μL 100 mM CaCl2 (per sample and/or digestion time point), diluted 1:10 from a 1 M stock, with gentle vortexing and immediately replace the tube in the 0 °C block 2 µL, 100 mM CaCl2 (per sample or digestion time point) 0 °C

steps.title: Incubate at 0 °C for the...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 0 °C for the desired digestion time (default is 30 min). 00:30:00, Incubation °C, Incubation

steps.title: Add 100 μL 2X STOP Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL 2X STOP Buffer and mix by gentle vortexing. When there are multiple digestion time points, remove 100 μL and add to a new tube containing 100 μL 2X STOP Buffer and mix by gentle vortexing. 100 µL, 2XSTOP

steps.title: Incubate 30 min @ 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate 30 min @ 37 °C to release CUT&RUN fragments from the insoluble nuclear chromatin. 00:30:00, Incubation 37 °C, Incubation

steps.title: Place on the magnet stand to clear
steps.instruction: Place on the magnet stand to clear. Cleanly transfer the supernatant containing digested chromatin to a fresh 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube and proceed with Phenol Chloroform Extraction (Step 57). 200 µL, STOP w/ Chromatin

steps.title: Remove liquid from the cap and...
steps.instruction: Remove liquid from the cap and side with a quick pulse on a micro-centrifuge. Place on the magnet stand to clear (~30 s) and pull off all of the liquid. 00:00:30, Magnet stand
